title: MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES - 211info
text: MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient services including alcohol and drug treatment, co-occurring disorder care, DUI programming, gambling treatment, mental health counseling, and cultural activities, with cultural and spiritual wellness guiding all service planning. Services include assessments, individual and group therapy, and crisis and safety planning, with referrals to community partners for medication management or specialized mental health needs. Works with community agencies to support participants involved in courts and social service systems, helping with re-entry and reducing barriers related to housing, employment, education, stigma, and rebuilding trust. Offers recidivism risk assessments, Rent Well classes, life-skills and employment readiness groups, parenting support, resource navigation, and volunteer opportunities. 58147 Columbia River Highway Suite C Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Provides culturally specific services for the Native American community. Some services are gender-specific, while other services are co-ed. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm DUII treatment: Monday-Friday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 396-5322 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 396-5322 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or to be placed on the waitlist FEES: Accepts Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and private insurance, income-based sliding scale fee is available DOCUMENTS: Photo ID, insurance card (if applicable), tribal ID (if applicable), discharge papers (if involved with another outpatient agency) AREA SERVED: -Non-culturally specific services: Columbia County -Culturally specific Native American service: Nationwide SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: On Columbia County Rider line Services: Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment * Native American Community DUI Offender Programs DUI Offender Programs * Native American Community Ex-Offender Reentry Programs Ex-Offender Reentry Programs * Native American Community Gambling Disorder Counseling * Native American Community Integrated Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment Integrated Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment * Native American Community If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
